note: Sample data. Deadlines are estimates based on typical annual cycles — always verify on the source site before applying.

opportunities.title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
opportunities.description: Fully funded scholarships for academically talented yet economically disadvantaged African students. Covers tuition, accommodation, and living expenses across 30+ partner universities globally.

opportunities.title: Chevening Scholarships — UK Masters
opportunities.description: A fully funded one-year masters scholarship in the UK. Covers tuition, living stipend, flights, and visa. Applications typically open August–November each year.

opportunities.title: DAAD EPOS Development-Related Masters
opportunities.tags: Fully funded, Masters, No IELTS required for some programs
opportunities.description: Fully funded masters scholarships in Germany for professionals from developing countries. Most programs taught in English; some do not require IELTS/TOEFL if your prior degree was in English.

opportunities.title: Commonwealth Shared Scholarships
opportunities.description: Fully funded masters scholarships at UK universities for candidates from low and middle income Commonwealth countries who could not otherwise afford to study in the UK.

opportunities.title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters
opportunities.description: Two-year masters programs delivered by consortiums of European universities. Full scholarships cover tuition, travel, and monthly stipend. Study in at least two different countries.

opportunities.title: UN Millennium Fellowship
opportunities.eligibility_year: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Final Year
opportunities.description: A semester-long leadership development program for undergraduates working on projects that advance the UN Sustainable Development Goals. Highly selective — under 2% acceptance rate.

opportunities.title: Mandela Washington Fellowship for Young African Leaders
opportunities.description: Flagship YALI program bringing 700 young African leaders annually to the US for six weeks of leadership training at American universities. Fully funded.

opportunities.title: Obama Foundation Leaders Africa
opportunities.description: Six-month leadership development program for rising African changemakers. Includes a week-long in-person convening plus virtual programming.

opportunities.title: Ashoka Youth Venturer
opportunities.description: Recognition and support program for young social entrepreneurs under 20 who are leading their own social change projects. Provides mentorship, networking, and a small stipend.

opportunities.title: Rise — Schmidt Futures & Rhodes Trust
opportunities.description: Lifetime support for young people aged 15–17 who need and deserve opportunity. Includes scholarships, mentorship, career development, and funding for their ideas.

opportunities.title: One Young World Summit Scholarships
opportunities.eligibility_year: Year 3, Year 4, Final Year, Recent Grad
opportunities.description: Attend the annual global summit alongside 2,000 young leaders from 190+ countries. Multiple scholarship tracks cover travel, accommodation, and summit access.

opportunities.title: Google STEP Internship
opportunities.description: 12-week paid summer internship for first and second year university students studying computer science. Designed for students early in their degree.

opportunities.title: Microsoft LEAP Apprenticeship
opportunities.description: 16-week paid apprenticeship preparing non-traditional candidates for tech careers at Microsoft. Many participants receive full-time offers.

opportunities.title: Google Summer of Code
opportunities.description: A global, paid, remote program where students contribute to open source projects over the summer. Stipend varies by country cost-of-living.

opportunities.title: UN Remote Internship Programme
opportunities.eligibility_year: Year 3, Year 4, Final Year, Recent Grad
opportunities.description: Volunteer remote internships across UN agencies. Usually 2–6 months, unpaid, but excellent experience and references. Roll openings listed on UN Careers portal.

opportunities.title: Andela Technical Leadership Program
opportunities.eligibility_year: Year 3, Year 4, Final Year, Recent Grad
opportunities.description: Technical training and career pipeline for African software engineers. Combines paid work with global companies and continued learning.

opportunities.title: ALX Software Engineering Program
opportunities.description: 12-month free intensive software engineering program for African youth. Graduates join ALX's placement pipeline with global employers.

opportunities.title: Graduate Roles at ALU / Center for Re-Imagined Africa
opportunities.description: Ongoing entry-level and early-career openings across ALU, its Centers of Excellence, and the ALU alumni network.

opportunities.title: Entry-Level Development Sector Roles
opportunities.description: Curated entry-level development sector openings across UN agencies, international NGOs, and development consultancies. Filter by 'Junior' or '0–2 years experience'.

opportunities.title: Hult Prize — Student Social Entrepreneurship Challenge
opportunities.eligibility_year: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Final Year
opportunities.description: The world's largest student competition for social good. Teams of 3–4 students pitch business ideas addressing a global challenge; winning team receives USD 1M in seed funding.

opportunities.title: Anzisha Prize for Young African Entrepreneurs
opportunities.description: Africa's premier award for young entrepreneurs aged 15–22 who are running their own businesses. Winners receive funding, mentorship, and exposure.

opportunities.title: Tony Elumelu Foundation Entrepreneurship Programme
opportunities.description: The largest African philanthropic initiative for entrepreneurs. Provides USD 5,000 seed capital, training, mentorship, and networking for 1,000 African entrepreneurs each year.

opportunities.title: Africa's Business Heroes
opportunities.description: Annual prize competition awarding USD 1.5M to African entrepreneurs, plus training and mentorship. Open to founders of businesses registered and operating in Africa.
